# Pennington Examples — Issue Remediation Plan

**Source of truth**: `issues.md` (regenerated by `tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples`)

Totals to beat: **2,377 errors · 13,140 warnings · 28 info** across 20 examples. Only 5 examples built clean on pass A; **zero** built clean on pass B.

## How to read and use this plan

Phases are ordered by leverage: earlier phases fix the most issues per unit of work. Each phase is **self-contained** — an agent can pick up any phase without having completed earlier ones (results compose, they don't depend). After any phase, re-run the validator to measure impact:

```
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples
# or for a single example:
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples -- --only <ExampleName>
```

The tool rewrites `issues.md` in place. Compare cross-example pattern counts before/after to verify impact.

### Issue-code legend (from `Validators.cs`)

| Prefix | Meaning | Source |
|--------|---------|--------|
| `L.*`  | Link integrity (href/src resolves to an emitted file) | `LinkValidator` |
| `T.*`  | TOC consistency, duplicates, orphans | `TocValidator` |
| `B.*`  | Base-URL prefixing (pass B only) | `BaseHrefValidator` |
| `S.*`  | `search-index.json` sanity | `SearchIndexValidator` |
| `M.*`  | `llms.txt` + `_llms/*.md` sanity | `LlmsTxtValidator` |
| `X.*`  | `sitemap.xml` sanity | `SitemapValidator` |
| `P.*`  | SPA page-data files | `SpaDataValidator` |
| `R.*`  | Engine's own `BuildReport` output scraped from stdout | `BuildReportScraper` |

Pass A = `base-url=/`, Pass B = `base-url=/preview/`. Every phase should verify *both* passes still validate after the fix.

---

## Phase 1 — Kill the `_content/Pennington.UI/*.js` file-lock error (ENGINE)

> ✅ **Done 2026-04-11.** Delivered via two engine changes in
> `src/Pennington/Generation/OutputGenerationService.cs` and
> `src/Pennington/Infrastructure/LinkVerificationService.cs`. Measured impact:
> `R.BUILD_FAILED` 35 → 26, `R.PAGE_FAILED` 106 → 24, `R.BROKEN_LINK` **13,127 → 1,681**
> (-11,446, -87%). Six examples now pass cleanly in both passes
> (`PrismDocsExample`, `TempoDocsExample`, `SpaNavigationExample`,
> `SpaNavigationTutorialExample`, `SearchExample`, `UserInterfaceExample`).
> Remaining `R.BUILD_FAILED` / `R.PAGE_FAILED` occurrences are legitimate
> content bugs belonging to Phases 2–7. See commit for details; root cause was
> not a copy-loop race but `DiscoverMapGetRoutes` failing to filter `MapStaticAssets`
> endpoints (which leaked into Phase 7 parallel fetching and overwrote Phase 4
> output), plus the link verifier's `/_content/` prefix check not being base-URL
> aware (which produced the 11k-warning cascade even after files were on disk).

**Leverage**: Gigantic. This single fault causes `R.BUILD_FAILED` on **every one of the 20 examples** (pass A + pass B = 35 occurrences) and cascades into most of the 13,127 `R.BROKEN_LINK` warnings because every page's `<script src="/_content/Pennington.UI/scripts.js">` dangles after the copy fails.

### Symptom (reproduced in every example section of issues.md)

```
#### ISSUE-0008 · R.PAGE_FAILED · error · pass A
- File: /_content/Pennington.UI/scripts.58q6579mip.js
- Message: The process cannot access the file
  'B:\Penn\tmp\validate-examples\AlexBlogExample\root\_content\Pennington.UI\scripts.58q6579mip.js'
  because it is being used by another process.
```

The same error recurs for:
- `/_content/Pennington.UI/scripts.js`
- `/_content/Pennington.UI/scripts.58q6579mip.js` (fingerprinted variant)
- `/_content/Pennington.UI/spa-engine.js`
- `/_content/Pennington.UI/spa-engine.5znfh79gun.js` (fingerprinted variant)
- And occasionally *content pages themselves* (e.g. `NorthwindHandbookExample/changelog/v2-0-0/index.html`, `YogaStudioExample/about/index.html`) — meaning the race is not limited to RCL static assets.

### Why this is a race

ASP.NET's static asset fingerprinting exposes **both** `scripts.js` and `scripts.<hash>.js` as distinct entries from the same file provider. The output pipeline walks the file provider and emits each entry; on Windows, two parallel tasks reading or writing the same underlying physical source (or the same target file under different names while an earlier copy's handle is still draining) produces `ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION`.

### Files to investigate

- `src/Pennington/Generation/OutputGenerationService.cs`
  - `CopyStaticAssetsAsync()` — starting point
  - `CopyFileProvider()` (~line 353) — recursive walker; check whether it uses `Task.WhenAll` or any parallelism
  - `CopyFile()` (~line 338) — the actual copy; currently calls `IFileSystem.File.Copy(source, target, true)`
- Also check whether HTTP-crawled pages (content routes) and asset copying share a `ParallelOptions` / `Parallel.ForEachAsync` / `Task.WhenAll` fan-out that lets a content page's `<script>` fetch race the file-system copy of the same asset.

### Investigation plan

1. **Prove the race**: add temporary logging that records, for each copied asset, `{source, target, thread-id, tick}`. Run a failing example (e.g. `--only AlexBlogExample`) and look for two log lines for the same source within milliseconds.
2. **Inspect fan-out**: grep `CopyStaticAssetsAsync` for `Task.WhenAll`, `Parallel.ForEachAsync`, `Parallel.ForEach`, `SemaphoreSlim`. The current call should be serializing asset copies; if it isn't, that's the bug.
3. **Check fingerprint duplication**: in a small repro, enumerate `IWebHostEnvironment.WebRootFileProvider` and any `CompositeFileProvider` contributors. Count how many times `scripts.js` appears across providers (fingerprinted + unfingerprinted both resolve via `ManifestEmbeddedFileProvider` for RCL assets in 8.0+). If it appears twice under the same target filename, the copy loop writes twice.
4. **Reconsider HTTP-crawl + copy interleaving**: the engine builds by running Kestrel in-process and fetching pages. If `CopyStaticAssetsAsync` runs *concurrently* with `GeneratePagesAsync`, a page-handler reading an asset from disk might still hold the file while the copier tries to overwrite it. Confirm whether these phases overlap.

### Fix options (pick one after investigation; do not guess)

- **Serialize asset copy per source path**: dedup the enumeration by *source* (not by target name), so `scripts.js` copies once; then emit any additional target names (fingerprinted aliases) as post-copy hardlinks or re-copies from the already-written target.
- **Sequence the phases**: ensure `CopyStaticAssetsAsync` runs to completion *before* any HTTP-crawl that could touch the same files. Put a `SemaphoreSlim(1,1)` around any write path into `_content/`, or move asset copy out of the crawl pipeline entirely.
- **`FileShare.ReadWrite | FileShare.Delete` on the target open**: last resort — only if the race is an unavoidable OS-level pattern. Prefer the serialization fixes above.

### Verification

- Re-run the validator. Expect: `R.BUILD_FAILED` and `R.PAGE_FAILED` for any `_content/Pennington.UI/*.js` path to go to **zero** across all 20 examples.
- Cascading `R.BROKEN_LINK` warnings pointing at `/preview/_content/Pennington.UI/scripts.js` etc. should also collapse (the engine's verifier now finds the file).
- `PrismDocsExample`, `TempoDocsExample`, `SearchExample`, `LocalizationExample`, `LocalizationTutorialExample` should all go from `pass B = fail` → `pass B = ok`.

### Estimated impact

- `R.BUILD_FAILED`: 35 → 0
- `R.PAGE_FAILED` for framework assets: ~60 → 0
- `R.BROKEN_LINK` cascades for framework assets: several thousand → 0 (the biggest chunk of the 13,127 warning total)

---

## Phase 2 — Fix `LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor` × `BaseUrlRewritingProcessor` interaction (ENGINE)

**Leverage**: Eliminates the entire `B.NOT_REWRITTEN` category (353 raw → 51 groups across 3 examples) plus the corresponding cascade in `L.BROKEN` for the same URLs in pass B.

### Symptom

`LocalizationExample` pass B, `issues.md` ISSUE-0138 and siblings:

```
- Selector: div.sticky > nav > ul.flex > li.block > a.block
- Message: href `/kl/preview/kl/about/` is not prefixed with base URL `/preview/`.
- Excerpt: <a href="/kl/preview/kl/about/" ...>
```

The href `/kl/preview/kl/about/` is visibly wrong: `/preview/` should come first, giving `/preview/kl/about/`. Same pattern for every locale (`pi`, `pl`, `sv`), same pattern in `YogaStudioExample/gen-z/*`, same pattern in `LocalizationTutorialExample`.

### Root cause (verified from processor ordering)

Processors run in ascending `Order` in the response pipeline (confirmed from `PenningtonExtensions.cs` ~line 149):

1. `XrefResolvingProcessor` (Order: **-10**) — resolves `xref:` UIDs to canonical routes (`/about/`)
2. `BaseUrlRewritingProcessor` (Order: **0**) — turns `/about/` into `/preview/about/`
3. `LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor` (Order: **50**) — *appends* current locale (`kl`) onto internal links

By step 3, the link is already `/preview/about/`. The locale rewriter sees a root-relative internal link and prepends `/kl/`, producing `/kl/preview/about/`. Then for pages *already* inside a locale subtree (where the pre-rewrite link was `/kl/about/`), step 2 produced `/preview/kl/about/` and step 3 prepends `/kl/` *again*, producing `/kl/preview/kl/about/`. Both shapes appear in `issues.md`.

### Files to modify

- `src/Pennington/Localization/LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor.cs` — primary fix site
- `src/Pennington/Infrastructure/BaseUrlRewritingProcessor.cs` — read-only reference for how it strips/applies prefixes
- `src/Pennington/Infrastructure/PenningtonExtensions.cs` (~line 149) — processor registration, possibly reorder

### Fix (recommended approach)

Make `LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor` **base-URL-aware**. On each link:

1. Strip any base-URL prefix to get the logical path (so `/preview/about/` → `/about/`).
2. Decide whether the logical path already starts with a known locale segment (`/kl/…`). If so, leave it alone — it's already locale-qualified.
3. Otherwise, prepend the current locale: `/about/` → `/kl/about/`.
4. Re-apply the base URL: `/kl/about/` → `/preview/kl/about/`.

Inject the base URL (`IOptions<PenningtonOptions>` or whatever the base URL source is — check how `BaseUrlRewritingProcessor` gets it) into `LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor`.

**Alternative (simpler but less clean)**: reorder processors so `LocaleLinkRewritingProcessor.Order = -20` (runs *before* `BaseUrlRewritingProcessor`). Then `BaseUrlRewritingProcessor` sees `/kl/about/` and produces `/preview/kl/about/` correctly. **Caveat**: verify nothing downstream depends on seeing the base URL when the locale processor runs. Write a test for both passes before changing order.

### Known locale list

Locales used in examples (derived from `issues.md` selectors):
- `LocalizationExample`: `en` (default), `kl`, `pi`, `pl`, `sv`
- `YogaStudioExample`: default + `gen-z`
- `LocalizationTutorialExample`: check `examples/LocalizationTutorialExample/Program.cs` for the configured locales

### Pass-A regression to fix alongside

`LocalizationExample` pass A also emits ISSUE-0133..0137:

```
- File: 404.html
- Message: Link `/__pennington-404-generator/` → missing target `/__pennington-404-generator/`.
```

The language-switcher dropdown on `404.html` links to `/<locale>/__pennington-404-generator/`, which is an internal marker route that never actually produces a file. This is probably related: the 404 page template is emitting a placeholder URL that was never substituted for the real landing page. Check `LocalizationExample`'s 404 template and whatever emits the language switcher. Likely either:
- The language switcher should link to `/<locale>/` (the landing page), not the generator route, or
- The generator route should produce a real 404 file at the target path.

This affects 5 + 5 = 10 occurrences per pass × 2 passes. Fold it into this phase since the fix area is close.

### Verification

- Re-run validator for `LocalizationExample`, `LocalizationTutorialExample`, `YogaStudioExample` in both passes.
- Expect `B.NOT_REWRITTEN` → 0 and the dependent `L.BROKEN` with `/kl/preview/…` shapes → 0.
- Expect `LocalizationExample` pass B exit code → 0.

### Estimated impact

- `B.NOT_REWRITTEN`: 353 → 0
- `L.BROKEN` in these three examples: drops by several hundred raw occurrences
- Pass B build of `LocalizationExample`, `LocalizationTutorialExample`, `YogaStudioExample`: should now succeed (once phase 1 is also applied for file-lock).

---

## Phase 3 — Relative-link resolution in markdown (ENGINE)

**Leverage**: Eliminates ~237 + ~163 + several dozen smaller `L.BROKEN` groups, primarily in `SpectreConsoleExample` and `MinimalExample`.

### Symptom A — `.md` links never rewritten

`SpectreConsoleExample` ISSUE-0456, 0457, 0458, 0459, 0460, 0474, 0475, 0476, 0477, 0478:

```
- File: console/tutorials/interactive-prompt-and-dashboard-tutorial/index.html
- Message: Link `../how-to/organizing-layout-with-panels-and-grids.md`
  → missing target `/console/tutorials/how-to/organizing-layout-with-panels-and-grids.md`.
```

The rendered HTML still contains `<a href="../how-to/foo.md">` — the `.md` suffix was never rewritten to the canonical URL (`/console/tutorials/how-to/foo/`).

### Symptom B — relative path from directory-style URL

`MinimalExample` ISSUE-0243..0246:

```
- File: sub-folder/page-one/index.html
- Message: Link `sample-post` → missing target `/sub-folder/page-one/sample-post`.
  Excerpt: <a href="sample-post">Sample Post with Images</a>

- File: sub-folder/page-two/index.html
- Message: Link `../` → missing target `/sub-folder/`.
- Message: Link `page-one` → missing target `/sub-folder/page-two/page-one`.
```

Source markdown at `sub-folder/page-one.md` (file) wrote sibling link `sample-post`, expecting it to resolve to the sibling file `sub-folder/sample-post.md`. But the canonical URL of `page-one.md` is the directory-style `/sub-folder/page-one/`, so the browser resolves `sample-post` as `/sub-folder/page-one/sample-post` — wrong tree level. Either the engine should *rewrite* relative `.md` sibling links at markdown-parse time using the **source file's directory**, or the example's author should have written `../sample-post/`.

### Symptom C — relative image src

`BeaconDocsExample` ISSUE-0043:

```
- File: getting-started/index/index.html
- Message: Image src `./beacon-arch.png`
  → missing target `/getting-started/index/beacon-arch.png`.
```

The image file is collocated with the source `.md` file, but after slug-to-directory conversion, the relative `./` no longer points at the right spot.

### Files to investigate

- `src/Pennington/Markdown/` — Markdig extensions and the markdown content parser
- Specifically: the Markdig pipeline configured in `MarkdownPipelineFactory.cs` and `MarkdownContentParser.cs` / `MarkdownContentRenderer.cs` under `src/Pennington/Markdown/`
- Check whether there is an existing `LinkInlineRenderer` override that rewrites `.md` → canonical URL
- `src/Pennington/Infrastructure/XrefResolvingService.cs` — handles `xref:` but *not* plain relative `.md` links; may be the right place to extend, or a new `RelativeMdLinkRewriter` may be needed

### Fix approach

At markdown-render time (not HTML-post-process time), every relative link inside a markdown body should be resolved against the **source file's directory in the content tree**, not against the rendered HTML's canonical URL. Concretely, when rendering `sub-folder/page-one.md`:

- `sample-post` (no extension) → attempt `.md` lookup in sibling directory → find `sub-folder/sample-post.md` → emit canonical URL `/sub-folder/sample-post/`.
- `../how-to/foo.md` → resolve to `console/tutorials/how-to/foo.md` from source path `console/tutorials/interactive-prompt-and-dashboard-tutorial.md` → emit `/console/tutorials/how-to/foo/`.
- `./beacon-arch.png` → resolve against source dir and emit a static-asset reference.

The engine already knows every source file → canonical URL mapping (it built the output). Adding a `ContentRoute`-aware link rewriter at the Markdig `LinkInline` render stage is the natural place. Look at how `XrefResolvingService` walks the DOM — you want something similar but inside the markdown pipeline, before HTML is generated.

### Verification

- `SpectreConsoleExample`: `console/tutorials/interactive-prompt-and-dashboard-tutorial/index.html` should no longer contain `.md` href suffixes; `L.BROKEN` count for this file → 0.
- `MinimalExample`: `sub-folder/page-one/index.html` and `page-two/index.html` resolve cleanly.
- `BeaconDocsExample`: the `./beacon-arch.png` image reference resolves — either by rewriting the link or by asking the example author to move the image.

### Estimated impact

- `L.BROKEN` errors: several hundred eliminated (primarily the `.md`-suffix group in SpectreConsoleExample)
- `R.BROKEN_LINK` engine warnings: similar reduction

---

## Phase 4 — `search-index.json` and `llms.txt` generation for `SearchExample` and `MinimalExample` (ENGINE)

**Leverage**: Medium — only a few examples affected, but the bugs expose whole content-source types that the search/llms builders aren't indexing.

### Symptom — empty search index despite 1001 pages

`SearchExample` ISSUE-0407, 0411:

```
- File: search-index.json
- Message: search-index.json is an empty array.
```

Pass A built 1001 HTML pages but the search index is empty. The example loads pages from a programmatically-generated content source (random routes like `/random/navigating/back-end bandwidth/sensor/g2jclnal9o/`). These are never enqueued for search indexing.

### Symptom — empty title/body

`MinimalExample` ISSUE-0252:

```
- File: search-index.json
- Selector: [1]
- Message: search-index entry #1 missing/empty: title, body (title=, url=/sub-folder/page-1/).
```

One entry has an empty title and empty body. `/sub-folder/page-1/` is also a curious URL that doesn't resolve (note: different from `/sub-folder/page-one/`). Likely an orphan route generated by some redirect/alias that surfaces in the TOC but has no rendered HTML content.

### Symptom — empty llms.txt

`SearchExample` ISSUE-0406, 0409:

```
- File: llms.txt
- Message: llms.txt contains no markdown link entries.
```

Same root cause family: the content enumeration used to build `llms.txt` isn't picking up programmatically-generated content.

### Files to investigate

- `src/Pennington/Search/SearchIndexService.cs` — entrypoint
- `src/Pennington/Search/SearchIndexBuilder.cs` — per-page builder
- `src/Pennington/LlmsTxt/LlmsTxtService.cs` — llms.txt entrypoint
- `src/Pennington/LlmsTxt/LlmsTxtContentService.cs` — build-time writer
- `examples/SearchExample/Program.cs` — how the example registers its 1001 pages (what `IContentService` or content source does it use?)
- `examples/MinimalExample/Program.cs` — where does `/sub-folder/page-1/` come from?

### Investigation plan

1. Start with `SearchExample`. Read `Program.cs` and identify which content service registers the random pages. The search index service likely enumerates only `MarkdownContentService` pages (or walks the TOC), missing custom content services.
2. Trace `SearchIndexService.BuildIndexAsync` (or equivalent). It probably iterates `IEnumerable<IContentService>` and, for each, calls `GetAllItems()` or similar. Check whether the custom content source in `SearchExample` actually implements that contract.
3. For `MinimalExample`, find what publishes `/sub-folder/page-1/`. Likely an `IContentService` that returns a stub `ContentItem` with no title/body — either a redirect placeholder or a misconfigured alias. Fix by either filtering those out of the search index (if they're intentional redirects) or by giving them proper content (if they're real pages).
4. For `llms.txt` in `SearchExample`, same root cause as search — the builder enumerates the wrong set.

### Fix approach

- Make search index and llms.txt builders enumerate *all* registered `IContentService` implementations uniformly, via whatever canonical `GetAll`/`GetItems` interface exists. If such an interface doesn't exist yet, that's the missing abstraction.
- Filter out items that don't have rendered HTML yet (redirects, placeholders) to avoid `S.MISSING_FIELD` false positives.

### Verification

- `SearchExample`: `S.EMPTY` → 0 (the index should contain ~1001 entries); `M.NO_ENTRIES` → 0.
- `MinimalExample`: `S.MISSING_FIELD` → 0 (either the orphan page gets real content, or it's filtered out of the index).

### Estimated impact

- `S.EMPTY`: 2 → 0
- `S.MISSING_FIELD`: 2 → 0
- `M.NO_ENTRIES`: 2 → 0

---

## Phase 5 — `sitemap.xml` bad URL (ENGINE)

**Leverage**: Small (2 occurrences, 1 example), but it's a trivial fix.

### Symptom

`BeaconDocsExample` ISSUE-0042, 0049:

```
- File: sitemap.xml
- Message: sitemap <loc> `/https://beacon-docs.example.com` does not resolve to any output file.
```

The sitemap is prepending `/` to what is already an absolute URL, producing `/https://…`.

### Files to investigate

- `src/Pennington/Feeds/SitemapService.cs`
- `src/Pennington/Feeds/SitemapBuilder.cs`
- `examples/BeaconDocsExample/` — check whether the example sets `SiteUrl`/`BaseUri` to `https://beacon-docs.example.com` and whether that value is being incorrectly combined with the page path.

### Fix

Check the URL join logic in `SitemapBuilder`. If it's doing `"/" + url` or `Path.Combine("/", url)`, replace with `Uri` construction that respects absolute vs. relative input.

### Verification

- `BeaconDocsExample`: `X.BROKEN` → 0.

### Estimated impact

- `X.BROKEN`: 2 → 0

---

## Phase 6 — TOC duplicates in `NorthwindHandbookExample` (ENGINE or EXAMPLE)

**Leverage**: Mid-small — 132 raw occurrences collapsed to 6 groups, all in one example, but indicative of a real bug.

### Symptom

ISSUE-0311, 0312, 0313, 0320, 0321, 0322:

```
- Selector: ul.flex > li.block > ul.mt-4 > li.block > a.block
- Message: TOC lists `/changelog/v2-0-0/` more than once.
```

Same three changelog URLs appear twice in the TOC of every page (11 pages × 3 dupes × 2 passes = rolled up into 6 groups, 132 raw).

### Files to investigate

1. **Example first**: `examples/NorthwindHandbookExample/Program.cs` and any navigation config (`Content/navigation.yml`, frontmatter, or DI registration). Look for:
   - Content sources that overlap (e.g., both `Content/changelog/` and `Content/changelog/v2-0-0.md` registered separately).
   - Navigation config that lists the changelog items twice.
2. **Framework second**: `src/Pennington/Navigation/NavigationBuilder.cs`. Check whether it de-dupes by canonical URL before emitting.

### Fix

- If the example is registering two overlapping content sources, fix the example.
- If the framework is failing to de-dupe, add a distinct-by-canonical-URL pass in `NavigationBuilder` (defensive) so future misconfig doesn't produce double-listed TOCs.
- Probably do both.

### Verification

- `NorthwindHandbookExample`: `T.DUP` → 0 in both passes.

### Estimated impact

- `T.DUP`: 132 → 0

---

## Phase 7 — Per-example content fixes (EXAMPLES)

Each sub-task is an independent example-side fix. These can be done in parallel — no cross-dependencies. Do them **after** Phase 1 so the validator reports aren't drowning in file-lock noise.

### 7a — `AlexBlogExample` / `BlogExample` / `MaraBlogExample` / `ForgePortalExample` / `RecipeExample`: missing root index and missing tag pages

**Root cause (per blog example)**:
- Site header has `<a href="/">Site Title</a>` but no `/index.html` is generated — the blog has only post pages.
- Blog posts list tags as `<a href="/tags/cli">…`, but tag-index pages aren't generated (these blog templates aren't configured to emit tag pages).

**Fix options** (pick per example — consult the example's intent):
1. Add a root `index.md` for each blog that renders a listing.
2. Either generate tag index pages (check the docsite template, or add a simple tag listing content source), or remove the tag-link markup from the post template.

**Files**:
- Each `examples/*BlogExample/Content/` and `examples/*BlogExample/Components/` for the relevant Razor layouts.
- `src/Pennington.DocSite/` (or wherever blog templates live) if the fix should be shared across blog examples.

**Specific per-example bugs**:
- `BlogExample` ISSUE-0064: manual link to `/blog/2026/03/building-a-cli-part-1` but the post's actual URL is `/blog/building-a-cli-part-1/`. Fix the markdown link inside the post.
- `RecipeExample` ISSUE-0346: `sitemap.xml` generation hits `HTTP 500`. Investigate — this may belong in Phase 5 if it's a framework bug, not an example bug. Check `examples/RecipeExample/Content/` for a content item whose sitemap entry can't be built.

### 7b — `YogaStudioExample`: missing `/instructors/<name>`, `/schedule/<id>`, `/gen-z/blog/<slug>` pages

ISSUE-0520..0547. The homepage and `/schedule/` list class slots and instructors as hyperlinks to `/instructors/maya-chen` etc., but those detail pages aren't generated. Either:
- Generate the detail pages from the content data (check how yoga example is currently configured),
- Or strip the links and keep the grid as non-interactive.

Also fix the language switcher for `gen-z` — same bug as LocalizationExample's 404 generator links — see Phase 2 tail.

### 7c — `SpectreConsoleExample`: `/` and `/blog` header links

ISSUE-0454, 0455. The site header links to `/` and `/blog` but neither exists. Either create those pages or rework the header to point at a real landing page. High-count (237 + 163 occurrences rolled up into 2 groups). Fix **after** Phase 3 (because `.md` relative links also need engine fixes).

### 7d — `NorthwindHandbookExample`: `R.PAGE_FAILED` on content pages

ISSUE-0308..0310 fail to write `changelog/v2-0-0/index.html` etc. due to file-lock. Phase 1 should fix this automatically. If it doesn't, investigate whether the example registers two content sources that target the same output path.

### Verification

- Re-run `--only <ExampleName>` per example.
- Each example's pass-A error count should approach zero after Phase 7.

### Estimated impact

- `L.BROKEN` errors: several hundred eliminated across blog/recipe/yoga/spectre examples.

---

## Appendix A — Current cross-example pattern table (baseline)

Copied from `issues.md` for quick reference. Re-check after each phase to measure impact:

```
R.BUILD_FAILED  (error)   — 35     raw · 20 examples   [PHASE 1]
R.PAGE_FAILED   (error)   — 106    raw · 14 examples   [PHASE 1]
L.BROKEN        (error)   — 1,581  raw / 240 groups · 12 examples   [PHASES 2,3,7]
B.NOT_REWRITTEN (error)   — 353    raw / 51  groups · 3 examples    [PHASE 2]
T.ORPHAN        (error)   — 172    raw / 18 groups  · 2 examples    [PHASE 2 tail]
T.DUP           (error)   — 132    raw / 6  groups  · 1 example     [PHASE 6]
M.NO_ENTRIES    (error)   — 2      raw · 1 example                  [PHASE 4]
S.EMPTY         (error)   — 2      raw · 1 example                  [PHASE 4]
S.MISSING_FIELD (error)   — 2      raw · 1 example                  [PHASE 4]
X.BROKEN        (error)   — 2      raw · 1 example                  [PHASE 5]
R.BROKEN_LINK   (warning) — 13,127 raw / 3,533 groups · 20 examples [PHASE 1 cascade + 2,3,7]
X.EMPTY         (warning) — 2      raw · 1 example                  [PHASE 4]
B.MISSING_BODY_ATTR (warning) — 1  raw · 1 example (UserInterfaceExample, index.html) [minor, revisit after 1]
M.MISSING       (info)    — 28     raw · 14 examples                [not an error; informational that example didn't enable llms.txt]
```

## Appendix B — Per-example baseline (from `issues.md`)

| Example | A | B | Err | Warn | Primary phase(s) |
|--|--|--|--:|--:|--|
| AlexBlogExample | fail | fail | 36 | 43 | 1, 7a |
| BeaconDocsExample | fail | fail | 6 | 131 | 1, 3, 5 |
| BlogExample | fail | fail | 91 | 143 | 1, 7a |
| ForgePortalExample | fail | fail | 28 | 120 | 1, 7a |
| LocalizationExample | ok | fail | 507 | 535 | 1, 2 |
| LocalizationTutorialExample | ok | fail | 49 | 74 | 1, 2 |
| MaraBlogExample | fail | fail | 33 | 40 | 1, 7a |
| MinimalExample | fail | fail | 20 | 33 | 1, 3, 4 |
| MultipleContentSourceExample | fail | fail | 13 | 41 | 1 |
| NorthwindHandbookExample | fail | fail | 143 | 165 | 1, 6 |
| PrismDocsExample | ok | fail | 1 | 27 | 1 |
| RecipeExample | fail | fail | 52 | 63 | 1, 7a |
| RoslynIntegrationExample | fail | fail | 18 | 33 | 1 |
| SearchExample | ok | fail | 5 | 3002 | 1, 4 |
| SpaNavigationExample | fail | fail | 8 | 24 | 1 |
| SpaNavigationTutorialExample | fail | fail | 6 | 35 | 1 |
| SpectreConsoleExample | fail | fail | 817 | 7755 | 1, 3, 7c |
| TempoDocsExample | ok | fail | 1 | 46 | 1 |
| UserInterfaceExample | fail | fail | 9 | 25 | 1 |
| YogaStudioExample | fail | fail | 534 | 805 | 1, 2, 7b |

After Phase 1, expect most `fail` → `ok` in both columns for examples that currently only fail due to file-lock (PrismDocs, Tempo, Search, Prism, Localization both passes, SpaNavigation both passes, Multiple, Roslyn, UserInterface, Minimal pass A).

## Appendix C — Running the validator

```bash
# Full run, writes issues.md:
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples

# Single example:
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples -- --only YogaStudioExample

# Skip pass B (for faster iteration when not debugging base-URL issues):
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples -- --skip-b

# Skip build (reuse cached build logs from a previous run):
dotnet run --project tools/Pennington.ValidateExamples -- --no-build
```

The tool writes build logs under `tmp/validate-examples/<ExampleName>/{build-a.log, build-b.log}` and the emitted site under `tmp/validate-examples/<ExampleName>/{root, prefixed}`. Poke around those directories to see exactly what was (or wasn't) written.
